page: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It is powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor operating at 2.3 GHz, featuring 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs, which establishes a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core performance, PassMark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is highly competitive, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth ranking in the top 10%, while large-block read and write bandwidths fall within the top 25%. Single-core performance is strong, positioning in the top 25% across stress-ng and Geekbench evaluations. The hardware also excels in specialized tasks, achieving top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and Redis SET operations, where it sustains 131.64 million operations per second.

From a resource density perspective, the b3-256 offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing high qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in single-threaded tasks; both gzip compression and text generation for small and medium language models perform at average levels, indicating that sequential processing tasks do not fully leverage the massive core count of this hardware configuration.

This server is highly suited for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads. Appropriate deployment scenarios include large-scale software compilation, multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and parallel data processing. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores, fast local NVMe storage, and high memory bandwidth makes it a robust choice for containerized environments and multi-tenant hosting.
description: The OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server featuring an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and a 16 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for PassMark CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, particularly for small-block reads. While single-threaded gzip compression and LLM text generation perform at average levels, the server excels in parallel processing, software compilation, and ray tracing. The b3-256 is highly suited for multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and intensive computational workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and 16 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD for high-performance multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage for demanding parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core AMD EPYC Milan processor with dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 256 GB of RAM and a 400 GB local NVMe SSD.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and parallel computing.
